generalities.text: Main THE ENCYCLOPEDIA OF PURE MATERIA MEDICA By TIMOTHY F. ALLEN, A.M., M.D. Presented by Médi-T NATRUM BROMATUM. Sodium bromide, Nabr. Preparation , Triturations or solutions in distilled water. Authority. Dr. Laborde, Archiv de Physiol., 1869 (A. H. Z., 87, p. 46), effects of a dose of 16 grammes. ► Melancholy. ► Loss of will power. ► Mental indolence. ► Stupor on waking. ► Vertigo; the ground seems to waver under his feet. ► Lachrymation. ► Sneezing. ► Face pale. ► Salivation. ► Salty taste. ► Loss of sensibility in the throat. ► Thirst. ► Cough. ► Pulse small, rapid. ► Slow pulse. ► Ataxia. ► Suppression of reflex sensibility. ► Great desire to sleep.
